question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rateLimitBucket's refillGreedy strategy by sending exactly 500 requests per second (the requestsPerSecond limit) in a perfectly timed burst, consuming all tokens before legitimate users can access the node, effectively shutting down processing for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's default limit of 1 request per second by sending a single opcode request per second to the /api/v1/contracts/opcodes endpoint, completely monopolizing the opcode processing capacity and denying all other users access to opcode functionality?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the predictable tmpDir path (/tmp/web3) to create a named pipe (FIFO) at that path before node startup, causing FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() to block indefinitely when trying to delete the FIFO, hanging the node startup process?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync annotation's unbounded task queue by submitting a large number of async tasks that each hold references to large objects, causing heap exhaustion and an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot actuator liveness endpoint at /actuator/health/liveness by sending malformed HTTP requests that cause the health check to return DOWN, triggering Kubernetes to restart the node and taking it offline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot auto-configuration's database connection pool by sending requests that hold database connections open for the full requestTimeout duration (10 seconds), exhausting the connection pool and causing all subsequent requests to fail with connection timeout errors?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cleanup() does not validate the resolved tmpDir path against a whitelist, allowing an attacker who can influence the java.io.tmpdir JVM property to redirect cleanup to delete arbitrary directories, causing the node to crash?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LoggingFilter's DATA_PATTERN regex (\

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpringApplication.run() Spring Boot startup sequence by sending requests to the node before all beans are fully initialized, causing NullPointerExceptions in partially initialized beans that crash the application context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication's embedded Tomcat server by sending HTTP/1.1 requests with Connection: keep-alive headers that hold connections open indefinitely, exhausting the Tomcat thread pool and preventing the node from accepting new connections?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tmpDir cleanup sequence by creating a circular symlink structure inside /tmp/web3 before node startup, causing FileSystemUtils.deleteRecursively() to enter an infinite loop and hang the node startup process indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() method's floor division (Math.floorDiv(gas, GAS_SCALE_FACTOR)) by sending requests with gas values between 1 and 10000 (which return 0 tokens consumed), effectively bypassing the gas throttle entirely and executing unlimited EVM computations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() method in ThrottleManagerImpl by triggering an InvalidParametersException after gas tokens are consumed, causing the gasLimitBucket to have tokens restored via restore(request.getGas()), and by repeatedly triggering this path, artificially inflate the bucket beyond its capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync Spring async executor by sending requests that trigger async callbacks which themselves spawn additional async tasks, creating an unbounded recursive async task chain that exhausts the thread pool and crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LoggingFilter's ByteArrayOutputStream(content.length() / 4) allocation by sending a request with a 2GB body, causing the node to allocate a 512MB ByteArrayOutputStream on the heap and trigger an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot auto-configuration's Hibernate session factory initialization (HibernateConfiguration) by sending requests that trigger schema validation failures, causing the application context to fail and the node to crash?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cleanup() is not synchronized or protected by a lock, allowing two concurrent node startup processes to race on the same tmpDir, causing one to delete the directory created by the other and leaving both nodes in a broken state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MetricsFilter or MetricsConfiguration to generate an unbounded number of unique metric tag combinations (high cardinality attack) by varying request parameters, causing the Prometheus registry to consume all available heap memory and crash the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the JacksonConfiguration's ObjectMapper by sending a deeply nested JSON payload to the /api/v1/contracts/call endpoint, causing a StackOverflowError during JSON deserialization that crashes the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot embedded server's HTTP/2 multiplexing by opening a single connection and sending thousands of concurrent streams, each consuming a worker thread, exhausting the thread pool and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the /tmp/web3 directory being created with default permissions (potentially world-writable) by writing a large number of files into it during node operation, causing the next cleanup() call on restart to consume excessive I/O and delay node recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot auto-configuration's connection to external services (e.g., Hedera network nodes) by sending requests that cause the node to open many outbound connections, exhausting the OS file descriptor limit and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LoggingFilter's reorderFields() method with a crafted JSON string that causes the DATA_PATTERN regex to produce an extremely long replacement string, consuming excessive memory during string manipulation and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication's auto-configured error handling by sending requests that trigger unhandled exceptions in the GenericControllerAdvice, causing the error to propagate to the Spring DispatcherServlet and crash the application context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bucket4j SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on the gasLimitBucket by sending many concurrent requests that all contend on the same synchronized lock, causing thread starvation and effectively shutting down the node's request processing?
